title: erinus alpinus
intro: Erinus alpinus, also known as mountain toadflax, is a flowering plant that belongs to the family Plantaginaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Erinus alpinus include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Erinus alpinus is typically prepared as a tea or infusion, and can also be used in salves or ointments for topical applications.
